title: Welcome to Morning Reflections - What This Channel Is About
description:
A quick introduction to who I am and what you will find here. I am Rebecca Price - hospital chaplain, radio host on WGSP 1490 AM, and somebody who believes the ordinary moments carry extraordinary grace.

0:00 Intro
1:30 My story - chaplaincy and radio
4:00 What to expect on this channel
6:30 How faith and work intersect
8:00 Where to find me
tags: faith,chaplain,radio,Memphis,morning devotional,Rebecca Price,WGSP,inspiration

title: When Grief Shows Up Uninvited - A Chaplain's Perspective
description:
Grief does not wait for a convenient time. It arrives in the middle of a Tuesday, at the grocery store, in the car on the way home. This is what fifteen years of chaplaincy has taught me about sitting with loss.

0:00 Intro
2:00 The myth of grief stages
5:30 What grief actually looks like
9:00 Why we try to rush it
12:30 Practices for the hard days
16:00 You are not behind in your grief
tags: grief,chaplain,loss,healing,faith,compassion,pastoral care,Memphis

title: Saturday Yoga Flow for Busy Caregivers - 30 Minutes
description:
This is my Saturday morning routine before the radio show. Gentle yoga designed for people who carry a lot for others - caregivers, nurses, chaplains, teachers. No experience needed. Just a mat and a few quiet minutes.

0:00 Opening intention
2:00 Breath work
5:30 Gentle flow
22:00 Cool down
27:00 Closing reflection
tags: yoga,caregiver wellness,morning yoga,chaplain,self care,gentle yoga,faith,30 minute yoga

title: Interview with Rev. Angela Dawson - Chaplaincy at the Margins
description:
Rev. Angela Dawson, chaplain at Golden Heart Medical Center, joins me to talk about meeting people at their lowest and why presence matters more than answers. One of my favorite conversations of the year.

0:00 Intro
2:30 Angela's path to chaplaincy
8:00 The hardest part of the work
14:00 What sustains her
20:00 Advice for people supporting grieving loved ones
26:00 Where to find Angela
tags: chaplain interview,Angela Dawson,pastoral care,grief ministry,faith conversation,hospital chaplain

title: Morning Pages Practice - Why I Journal Every Day at 4:45 AM
description:
I have been doing morning pages for years. Three pages, longhand, before the world wakes up. It is not therapy and it is not prayer - it is something in between. Here is how I do it and why it matters.

0:00 What morning pages are
3:00 My 4:45 AM routine
6:30 How to start if you have never done it
9:00 What to do when you do not feel like it
11:30 The connection to faith practice
tags: morning pages,journaling,morning routine,faith practice,chaplain,self care,writing,devotional

title: Radio Clip: Pastor William Greer on Joy as Discipline
description: From this past Saturday's show on WGSP 1490 AM - Pastor William Greer of Grace Community Church shares one of the most grounded things I have heard on the subject of joy. This clip alone is worth 8 minutes of your morning.
tags: WGSP,radio clip,Pastor Greer,Grace Community Church,joy,faith,Memphis,morning reflections

title: 3 Things I Tell Every Family in the ICU Waiting Room #shorts
description: Fifteen years of chaplaincy distilled into three sentences. For anyone who has ever sat in that room.
tags: chaplain,ICU,hospital,grief,family support,shorts,faith

title: Compassion Fatigue Is Real - And Here Is What Helped Me
description:
Nobody talks about this enough in caregiving professions. Compassion fatigue nearly took me out five years in. This is not a clinical lecture - it is what it looked like in my own life and what I did about it.

0:00 What compassion fatigue actually feels like
4:00 The moment I knew something had to change
8:30 What helped - therapy, boundaries, community
13:00 Practices I still use
16:30 You cannot pour from empty
tags: compassion fatigue,chaplain,caregiver burnout,self care,boundaries,faith,healing,pastoral care

title: Saturday Yoga - Strength and Stillness Flow - 35 Minutes
description:
A longer Saturday morning practice for when you have a bit more time. Builds from grounding postures into something that opens the chest and shoulders - exactly what you need after a week of sitting with people in hard places.

0:00 Opening
3:00 Grounding sequence
10:00 Standing flow
22:00 Floor work
30:00 Savasana and closing reflection
tags: yoga,caregiver wellness,Saturday yoga,chaplain self care,strength yoga,morning yoga,35 minute yoga

title: New Year Sunrise Reflection 2026 - Light in the Darkness
description:
The meditation I delivered at Grace Community Church's New Year sunrise service this year. Cooper-Young neighborhood, Memphis TN, 6:15 AM. The text is John 1. The theme: what do we do with a light that the darkness cannot overcome.

Thank you to everyone who asked for this to be posted. This one lives in my bones.
tags: New Year,new beginnings,Grace Community Church,Memphis,sunrise service,sermon reflection,John 1,faith

title: One Prayer for the ICU Night Shift #shorts
description: For everyone who has ever kept watch through the night. You are not alone.
tags: prayer,ICU,night shift,chaplain,hospital,faith,shorts

title: Interview with Sandra Coles - Nursing, Faith, and Staying Whole
description:
My best friend Sandra Coles, nursing professor at Bluff City Community College, joins me for a conversation about what it means to sustain a caregiving vocation over decades. Sandra has been nursing for 25 years and she is sharper and kinder than ever. We recorded this on a Tuesday afternoon after Cafe Eclectic.

0:00 Intro
3:00 How Sandra got into nursing
9:00 What faith has meant in the work
16:00 The conversation nobody has with nursing students
23:00 What sustains her now
tags: nursing,Sandra Coles,caregiver,faith,Bluff City,interview,vocational calling,Memphis

title: Greenline Walk and Talk - Finding God in the Ordinary
description:
Took my phone to the Shelby Farms Greenline for a walking devotional. This is where I process most of what the week brings. The garden, the trail, the cat on the porch - these are my sanctuaries.

Filmed on a Thursday morning before prep work. Light is perfect at that hour.
tags: walking devotional,Greenline Memphis,nature prayer,ordinary sacred,faith practice,chaplain,outdoor meditation

title: Radio Clip: Deacon Charles Mosley on the Church Health Fair
description: From Saturday's show - Deacon Charles Mosley of Grace Community Church talks about the annual community health fair we co-organize every year. Why the church is a health access point and why that matters in Memphis.
tags: church health fair,Charles Mosley,Grace Community Church,Memphis health,community care,WGSP,radio clip

title: Saturday Yoga - Restorative Practice for Grief and Exhaustion - 25 Minutes
description:
This one is for the hard weeks. Fully restorative, mostly floor-based, focused on releasing what you have been holding. I recorded this the week after a particularly difficult stretch on the oncology ward.

0:00 Opening intention
3:00 Supported forward fold
8:00 Reclined twist
13:00 Legs up the wall
20:00 Rest and breath
24:00 Closing prayer
tags: restorative yoga,grief,exhaustion,chaplain self care,yoga for caregivers,25 minute yoga,morning yoga

title: What the Garden Teaches About Patience #shorts
description: My backyard this morning. Zinnias just coming up. Lavender already going strong. Some things cannot be rushed.
tags: garden,patience,faith,zinnias,lavender,Memphis,shorts,devotional

title: Seven Years After Divorce - What I Know Now
description:
I do not talk about my divorce much publicly. But I am 50 this year and looking back with some perspective. This is not a bitterness video and it is not a neat bow. It is honest.

0:00 Why I am talking about this now
3:00 The identity piece
7:30 What I got wrong
11:00 What held me
15:00 For anyone in the middle of it right now
tags: divorce,healing,identity,faith,single,resilience,women over 50,chaplain,personal story

title: What I Read Last Month - November Books and Why Faith Matters in Reading
description:
Currently reading Barbara Kingsolver again and working through Henri Nouwen's The Return of the Prodigal Son for the third time. Also picked up Brene Brown's newest. Here is what is landing for me.

0:00 Intro
2:00 Barbara Kingsolver - why her work stays with me
6:00 Henri Nouwen - what keeps drawing me back
10:30 Brene Brown and the courage conversation
14:00 What I am reading next
tags: books,reading,Barbara Kingsolver,Henri Nouwen,Brene Brown,faith and reading,book recommendation,chaplain

title: Honoring the Women of Faith - A Message for Complicated Mother Relationships
description:
Honoring our mothers is not simple for everyone. Some of you are grieving a mother. Some of you have a mother who is hard to love. Some of you are estranged. This message is for you - not just for the hallmark version.

Preached at Grace Community Church, December 2025.
tags: honoring mothers,complicated grief,healing,church,sermon,Grace Community,Memphis,family,faith

title: Radio Show Anniversary - 7 Years of Morning Reflections
description:
Seven years on WGSP 1490 AM this month. I started this show in 2019 with a borrowed microphone and a lot of nerve. Thank you to TJ Jackson, every sponsor who has believed in this work, and to you - the 8,000 to 12,000 listeners who show up every Saturday morning.

0:00 How the show started
5:00 What I have learned
10:00 Favorite guests and moments
17:00 What is coming this fall
tags: WGSP 1490,radio anniversary,Morning Reflections,TJ Jackson,Memphis radio,faith radio,7 years

title: My Cooper-Young Garden in Early January #shorts
description: The beds are resting under a thin frost this year. The rosemary is holding strong through the cold. The bare zinnia stalks are waiting on spring. Mercy the cat supervised the whole thing from the porch swing.
tags: garden,Cooper-Young,Memphis,zinnias,rosemary,frost,shorts,winter garden

title: Interview with Pastor William Greer - 50 Years of Ministry
description:
Pastor William Greer of Grace Community Church turns 72 this year. Fifty years in ministry. I have known him my entire adult life. This conversation took three hours and we cut it down to thirty minutes. Every minute stayed for a reason.

0:00 Intro
2:30 How he started
8:00 The Memphis he has seen change
14:00 What the church owes the neighborhood
21:00 What he would tell a new pastor
27:00 What sustains him
tags: Pastor Greer,Grace Community Church,Memphis ministry,50 years,faith interview,church community,WGSP

title: Saturday Yoga - Morning Energy Flow - 28 Minutes
description:
A slightly more energizing practice for Saturday mornings when you need to wake up before a big day. I use this on show mornings when I need to arrive at the Beale Street studio grounded and ready.

0:00 Opening breath
3:00 Sun salutations
12:00 Balance sequence
20:00 Cool down
26:00 Closing intention
tags: morning yoga,Saturday yoga,energy flow,caregiver,chaplain,28 minute yoga,morning routine,faith

title: Cooking Sunday Dinner Alone - A Single Person's Comfort
description:
I made pot roast, mac and cheese, cornbread, and peach cobbler this Sunday. The kids were not here. Dorothy came by in the afternoon. This is about what it means to feed yourself with care - and why Sunday dinner matters even when you are cooking for one.

No recipe video. Just a conversation over a stove.
tags: Sunday dinner,cooking alone,comfort food,pot roast,cornbread,peach cobbler,single life,Memphis,faith and food

title: Radio Clip: Covenant Insurance Group on Community Care
description: From last Saturday's show - a conversation with our sponsor Covenant Insurance Group about how faith communities can partner with local businesses to expand health access. Short clip from the 8 AM hour.
tags: WGSP,Covenant Insurance,community health,Memphis,radio clip,Morning Reflections,sponsor feature

title: What Mercy the Cat Taught Me About Rest #shorts
description: My five-year-old gray tabby Mercy has one speed on Sunday afternoons. There is a sermon in it.
tags: cat,rest,Sabbath,Mercy,faith,shorts,humor,devotional

title: CPE and Clinical Pastoral Education - What It Actually Is
description:
People ask me about my training all the time. Clinical Pastoral Education is not seminary - it is the clinical residency where you learn to be present with people in crisis. I did mine at Regional One Health in 2005-2007. Here is what it involves and how it shaped me.

0:00 What CPE is
3:30 My residency at Regional One
8:00 The hard cases that formed me
13:00 What it teaches about presence
17:00 How to pursue it if you are called
tags: CPE,clinical pastoral education,chaplaincy training,Regional One Health,hospital chaplain,pastoral care education

title: Brandi Carlile Changed My Relationship with Lament
description:
I do not usually do music commentary but I have been listening to Brandi Carlile for three years now and something needs to be said. The way she handles grief and beauty in the same breath - that is theology as far as I am concerned.

0:00 The album that started it
4:00 What lament is in the faith tradition
8:00 Why artists sometimes say it better than preachers
12:00 Recommendations if you want to start somewhere
tags: Brandi Carlile,music,lament,faith and music,grief,chaplain,Americana,folk,theology

title: Radio Clip: Covenant Insurance & Mitchell's Flowers - Sponsor Appreciation
description: Brief clip from the December sponsor segment - thanking Covenant Insurance Group, Mitchell's Flowers, and Bluff City Auto Care for their continued support of Morning Reflections. These businesses make local radio possible.
tags: WGSP,Morning Reflections,Covenant Insurance,Mitchell's Flowers,Bluff City Auto Care,radio,Memphis,sponsors

title: Upcoming: Community Church Health Fair - What to Expect
description:
Coming Saturday, October 3, 2026 - Grace Community Church's annual health fair, organized with Deacon Charles Mosley. Free screenings, community resources, and conversation. More details soon. If you want to volunteer or participate, reach out.

This is what the church is for.
tags: church health fair,Grace Community Church,Deacon Mosley,Memphis community,health access,faith and health,upcoming events
